TextENGNew: ``...Since we're being honest, let me say something serious.@k@vS14/06/220600206. I've seen it too.@k@vS14/06/220600207. ...One of those 'dreams' you mentioned, Rika-chama.``
``Well, we don't have time for the full story, so I'll skip the details.``
``...In that memory, more than 48 hours had actually passed since Rika-chama's death, yet none of the villagers had died.``
``If we can replicate those conditions, we might be able to disprove their so-called emergency manual.``
``What kind of... dream was it?``
``......It was a nightmare.@k@vS14/06/220600212. A dream in which I killed everyone.``
Yes. It was surely a dream, yet I can recall it with vivid clarity.
Consumed by my feelings for Satoshi-kun, I refused to trust a single soul in the village and spiraled further and further out of control.
First, Granny Oryou. Then Mayor Kimiyoshi. Then Rika-chama, Satoko, Mion, and Kei-chan...
In the end, I lost my mind completely and fell from my apartment building... and died. Something like that.
It was a future I never want to remember. Even now, when it surfaces in my dreams, my whole body trembles, sweat pours like a waterfall, and I'm seized by a terror that chills me to the bone.
But.
Just now, it hit me.
Back then -- I'm certain of it -- even 48 hours after I killed Rika-chama, I and everyone else were undeniably still alive.
What was different? ...Yes, that's it.
That's right -- until I confessed to Kei-chan and Rena that I had killed Rika-chama, I never told a soul what I had done.
And ironically... that served as clear proof that the enemies targeting Rika-chama required her 'confirmed death' before they could launch their operation --

``...Well, that's the situation.@k@vS14/11/220700255. The warrant for your arrest was issued by the court on charges of obstruction of justice.@k@vS14/11/220700256. We have to keep you in custody for 48 hours starting now, so do make yourself comfortable.``
changeReason: Original 'obstruction of government duties' vs new 'obstruction of justice.' JPN 公務執行妨害 specifically means obstruction of public duties/government operations, different from 'obstruction of justice.'

TextENGNew: ``Nfu fu fu fu!!``
Ooishi leaned his elbows on the table and peered at me intently as he said that. Of course, I knew he was teasing, so I kept eating my katsudon without pause, shoveling it in rather ungracefully.
Come to think of it, why is it always katsudon during interrogations? ...Well, I was hungry, and it was actually quite tasty, so I didn't mind -- though it was a bit heavy for a late-night meal.
``...What happened to the assault charge and the firearms violation?@k@vS14/06/220600214. They were declared quite emphatically.``

``Ohh, that.@k@vS14/11/220700259. ...Actually, Dr. Irie called.@k@vS14/11/220700260. He said you came to show him a modified model gun, and it accidentally went off due to the wrong gunpowder -- that's the story now.``
changeReason: Minor stylistic difference; both convey the cover story about a modified model gun and gunpowder issue. New adds 'that's the story now' for context.

TextENGNew: ``With both the victim and witnesses saying as much, there's nothing to report to the court.``
``Well... that doesn't really matter to me.@k@vS14/06/220600216. I was prepared for the consequences.``
``Don't be foolish.@k@vS14/11/220700263. Obstruction charges can be dropped at the precinct's discretion, but the other two are far more serious.``
``A criminal record is one thing, but I'm sure you wouldn't want to be separated from your friends.``
``...You're being surprisingly considerate.@k@vS14/06/220600218. I'm genuinely taken aback.``

``Nfu fu fu fu!!@k@vS14/11/220700266. Well, naturally I'd love nothing more than to throw a daughter of the hated Sonozaki family behind bars and rub that wrinkled old crone's and that insufferable young matriarch's noses in it.``
changeReason: Original 'bratty sister' vs new 'insufferable young matriarch.' JPN 姐御 means female boss/matriarch, not sister. Significantly different characterization.

TextENGNew: ``But you're putting your life on the line to protect the head of the Furude family. The least I can do is show some consideration.``
Ooishi said that with a light chuckle. His expression was as unreadable as ever, but even so, I could sense not a trace of ill will directed at me.
``...So, what happens now?@k@vS14/06/220600220. If you'd like to take a 'statement,' I'm prepared to 'cooperate' fully.``
changeReason: Original 'If you can get me written record of what I asked for, I'll do my best to cooperate' vs new 'If you'd like to take a statement, I'm prepared to cooperate fully.' Different meaning: original is demanding a document; new is offering to give a statement.

TextENGNew: ``No, no -- we're completely on the back foot this time.@k@vS14/11/220700269. After all, we're up against an unknown organization, and we have few allies on our side.``
``Without solid evidence, we can't make a move. We're entirely dependent on information from Akasaka-san.``
``My, my.@k@vS14/06/220600222. ...How utterly unreliable the arm of the state turns out to be.``
``Na ha ha ha! I'm ashamed to admit it.``
``...Well, setting that aside.``

Ooishi's smile suddenly vanished, and he exchanged a glance with Detective Kumagai standing behind him. Kumagai responded by picking up a file from the desk near the entrance and spreading it open in front of me.
``This is...@k@vS14/06/220600224. a photograph of Hinamizawa?``

``That's right.@k@vS14/11/220700274. ...Actually, these are developed from film found inside a bag that was turned in as lost property at the assembly hall on the night of the festival.@k@vS14/11/220700275. Quite a volume of photographs.``
changeReason: Original 'Along with... various other documents' vs new 'Quite a volume of photographs.' JPN 他にもいろんな書類 means 'various other documents,' which original captures; new changes to photographs.

TextENGNew: ``You don't mean--?!``

``Exactly as you suspect.@k@vS14/11/220700277. These undoubtedly belong to the missing Tomitake Jirou.@k@vS14/11/220700278. Moreover, this is extremely important evidence.@k@vS14/11/220700279. ...It's nothing short of a miracle that it was delivered here.``
According to Ooishi, various documents had been concealed within, disguised as rolls of film.

``There was quite a lot of information regarding 'Tokyo' -- specifically about the individuals funding and directing Takano Miyo, and their channels of communication.@k@vS14/11/220700281. I had an informant deliver the documents to Akasaka-san last night.``
``It likely won't be long before Tokyo's conspirators decide to pull out of Hinamizawa entirely.``
``Then... what about Rika-chama?``
``Yes, she should be safe.``

``...However, there is one thing that concerns me.@k@vS14/11/220700285. This building here -- Shion-san, does it look familiar to you?@k@vS14/11/220700286. I seem to have forgotten, and I can't quite place where it is.``

``This is...@k@vS14/06/220600228. Hmm~~, the shape of the mountain in the corner and the color of the building... I feel like I've seen it somewhere before...@k@vS14/06/220600229. But I can't quite place it.``
changeReason: Original ends with 'Hmm?' vs new ends with 'But I can't quite place it.' Different conclusion to the observation.

TextENGNew: ``What is it?``
``This metal pipe... there's something written on it.@k@vS14/06/220600231. Can you see it? Here, look.``
``Hmm...@k@vS14/11/220700289. Kuma-chan, can you enlarge this section?@k@vS14/11/220700290. Give it a try, would you?``
``Roger!!``

Just then, there was a knock at the door. Without waiting for a response, the door swung open, and as Detective Kumagai stepped out, an elderly man with a wild shock of white hair strode in to replace him.
``Easy there, old-timer. At least wait for a reply before you barge in.``
``Fool, there's no time for that!!@k@vS14/00/223000030. ...The autopsy results for Teppei are in.@k@vS14/00/223000031. No drugs or toxins detected in his blood.``
``Is that all?@k@vS14/11/220700293. A report like that can wait. Tell me later when there's time.``
``Let me finish!@k@vS14/00/223000033. Listen -- despite finding no drugs whatsoever, there was an injection mark on his neck that didn't line up with the throat wound.``

``Given the location of the mark and working backward from the timeline, the absence of any residual reaction is highly unnatural.@k@vS14/00/223000035. Furthermore, in the infected tissue around that wound, there was clearly a foreign hair caught in it.``
``...And the hair product coating it is a rather uncommon variety.@k@vS14/00/223000037. At the very least, it's not sold anywhere in this village!!``
``...Old-timer, can you identify the perpetrator from that?``

``DNA testing hasn't been introduced in Japan yet.@k@vS14/00/223000039. But when it comes to hair products, that's a different story!@k@vS14/00/223000040. If we can identify the specific product, we can narrow down who uses it.``
``After that, if we can determine who regularly uses that product, it becomes a powerful lead!!``
``Excellent work, old-timer!@k@vS14/11/220700296. With that evidence, the police can bring Takano's group in as persons of interest!@k@vS14/11/220700297. Magnificent!``
``Ga ha ha ha!@k@vS14/00/223000043. Think nothing of it...@k@vS14/00/223000044. Hmm? What's this -- a shed at the quarry?``
``--Oh, I know! This is Yagouchi!``
``Oh my, how did you figure that out, old-timer?!``
``Of course -- I used to spend plenty of time around there!@k@vS14/00/223000046. More importantly, what's this symbol...?``
``...Rika, are you all right?``
``Mii. ...I'm fine.@k@vS14/05/220500218. Probably.``
I kept my voice as low as possible while answering Satoko's muffled call from above.
I could hear the springs creaking, but for the moment, the ceiling pressing close above me -- the underside of the bed -- didn't seem in danger of collapsing.
``You're welcome to sleep if you like... but please don't snore.@k@vS14/04/220400068. If I hear so much as a peep, I'll kick the bed and wake you right up.``
``Mii~. Then if I hear you snoring, I'll kick the bed with everything I've got.@k@vS14/05/220500220. We'll see who snores less -- it's a competition~``
``I-I won't lose...!``
I stifled a laugh at Satoko's reply -- all bravado despite her obvious flustering.
Satoko, with her adorable little snores, was by far the more frequent offender. My victory was practically assured... but perhaps this time I'd let her win.
...Irie had arranged for Satoko and me to be secretly moved to a vacant examination room. I was lying on a mat beneath Satoko's bed, hidden behind stacks of medical supply boxes.

They would never suspect we were still right here, at the very place that had been attacked. ...It was Shion's idea. Later, around evening, we'd ride with Irie when he went to examine Oryou, and the two of us would slip into the Sonozaki house's underground room.
--About 30 hours to go.
I checked the small clock tucked under my arm and let out a soft sigh.
Just a little over a day. ...The second hand seemed to crawl with agonizing slowness. This would likely be the longest stretch of time in my entire life, carved out in this cramped space.
Would it work...? The anxiety was still overwhelming.
I had tried hiding in the mountains and elsewhere to escape the enemy's assault before, but it had never once succeeded.
...That said, this was the first time I had hidden with the club members' help, so part of me wanted to see how things would turn out. ...No, 'wanted to see' isn't right. It was more like hope.
On top of the familiar anxiety and fear, there was now a strange flutter of something resembling excitement. How peculiar.
...That was when it happened.
``Hey... Rika.@k@vS14/04/VT4Bc_satoko1026. Would you hear me out for a moment?``
Satoko, who had been silent for a while, suddenly spoke up. ...What was this about? Her tone was more rigid than before, carrying an oddly tense quality.
``What is it, Satoko?``
``There's something I want to tell you, Rika.@k@vS14/04/220400073. ...About that incident, three years ago.``
``Mii. ...Satoko,@k@vS14/05/220500224. it's best to put that behind you.``

``No, please listen.@k@vS14/04/220400075. The whole time I was asleep, I kept dreaming about it over and over.@k@vS14/04/220400076. And now I feel like if I don't get this out, I might never have another chance.``
That day, I went on a drive with my mom and my stepdad.
...I really didn't want to go.
He looked intimidating, yet he was strangely overfamiliar, always trying to ingratiate himself with me...
I was sure he was two-faced, just like all of Mom's previous boyfriends.
So I made up my mind that I would never let myself warm up to him.
But... what they call the weakness of love, I suppose.
Mom took his affected kindness at face value... and fell for him completely.
It was maddening. I couldn't stand it.
changeReason: Both convey frustration/maddening feelings; 'irritation and regret' vs 'maddening, couldn't stand it' are different phrasings of similar emotions.

TextENGNew: Mom was being deceived.
And before long, they would fight and grow apart... and then break up, leaving her miserable all over again.
So I... did what I always did. I harassed him in every way I could think of, trying to drive him away.
Surely that would strip away his facade. Once Mom saw through him, she'd come to her senses. ...After all, it had always worked that way before.
But... that man was different from the others.
Instead of getting angry, he would agonize over it, looking pained and sad... and late into the night, he and Mom would talk endlessly about me.
And that... only made me more uncomfortable.
He must be scheming something.
He was going to win Mom over, label me as a nuisance, and throw me out of the house.
Or maybe... maybe he was even planning to kill me and make it look like an accident...?
Those thoughts consumed me constantly.
So when they arrived at the destination of the drive and he said, 'Let's go look at the river together,'
...I was sure he meant to push me off the cliff. So I decided to stay behind in the car.
And then, alone in the car... a sudden wave of loneliness swept over me, followed by sadness, and then... fear.
Maybe... maybe I really was just a burden to them.
And maybe... the two of them were using this time alone to plan how to get rid of me.
The moment I thought that... I heard a horrible ringing deep in my ears, and my whole body started itching as if I'd been covered in insect bites.
When I came to, I had stepped out of the car... and from the shadows, I was watching the two of them standing at the edge of the cliff.
And then......
``...Are you all right, dear?@k@vS14/00/222700002. You came home late again last night.``
``Hmm? Oh, I'm fine.@k@vS14/00/222600002. I'm the only one who can mediate with the supporters, after all.@k@vS14/00/222600003. And your homemade bento gives me all the energy I need. Don't worry.``
``What a thankless role... honestly...``
``Ga ha ha ha! Well, I'm used to being hated!@k@vS14/00/222600005. With a mug this scary and a voice this gruff, it'd be stranger if people liked me!``
``Hee hee hee...@k@vS14/00/222700005. I felt the same way at first, you know.``
``Ha ha ha! I'll never forget how scared you looked the first time we met!``
He said that with a hearty laugh, his large frame shaking. ...But then, as if remembering something, his laughter faded, and he dropped his shoulders with a sigh.
``...But because of all that, I've caused poor Satoko-chan nothing but trouble...``
Satoko's mother gently placed her hand on the large man's shoulder beside her and peered into his face. He had hunched his massive frame, his shoulders slumped in a way that seemed wholly unfitting.

``A kindhearted wife and adorable children... That was my dream.@k@vS14/00/222600009. I'd all but given up, so when it all came together at once, I was so genuinely overjoyed that... I got carried away.``
``And that's probably why I caused all those misunderstandings with Satoko-chan.@k@vS14/00/222600011. ...The girl did nothing wrong. It was my mistake...``
``That's not...``

``...No amount of apology could ever be enough for that child.@k@vS14/00/222600016. So... once we receive the subsidy and move away, I want to make it up to her with everything I have.@k@vS14/00/222600017. I want to give both of those kids a proper life.``
changeReason: Original 'Plus... I've made things harder for Satoshi-kun and Satoko-chan... by fighting' vs new starts with different JPN content. TextENGNew contains substantially different content.

TextENGNew: ``...But with the supporters counting on me, I can't bend the knee and back down now...``
``It will be all right.@k@vS14/00/222700009. ...Both of them are strong children.@k@vS14/00/222700010. I believe they'll understand, someday.``
...Satoko couldn't see his face.
Even so... she could tell from the sound of his voice.
Satoko's stepfather... was crying.
``...I was the one at fault.@k@vS14/00/222700012. Every time a man flattered me or spoke kindly, I'd get swept up in it, desperate not to be disliked, and... I forgot about my own children.``
``I never even considered how badly Satoko wanted my attention...``

``...No amount of apology could ever be enough for her.@k@vS14/00/222600016. So... once we get the government subsidy and move, I want to make things right with everything I have.@k@vS14/00/222600017. I want to give those two children a real home.``
``Right...``
...After that, the two of them lost themselves in painting visions of their future, growing more and more animated.
Needless to say... every plan revolved around Satoko and Satoshi.
In the new house, they would make rooms for Satoko and Satoshi.
They would fill Satoko's room with adorable stuffed animals.
They would find a neighborhood baseball team for Satoshi to join... and on days off, they'd play catch together.
A big color television for the living room.
The latest video game console, so they could invite lots of friends over.
New bicycles to replace their worn-out old ones. Those newer models with gear shifts had just come out, hadn't they?
The mother and stepfather were split on whether to send them to cram school... but even that disagreement was overflowing with love for the two children.
A droplet fell to the ground, and she noticed it.
Satoko realized she was crying.
Why?
Why... why had she harbored such terrible thoughts about these people... when they were so incredibly kind?
She had been so cruel to them.
She had told so many horrible lies.
And yet, these people had...
...been her parents, all along. Through everything.
changeReason: Both say the person was a parent all along; 'daughter' vs 'parents' shifts perspective but same meaning.

TextENGNew: I'm sorry.
I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry...!
Satoko scrubbed at the tears streaming down her face and looked up at the two of them once more.
...Watching them talk so happily about the future must have lifted their spirits, because Satoko could feel the darkness that had clouded her heart begin to clear.
Let me try to believe.
In Mom, and in that man... no, in my stepdad.
Pretend I heard nothing.
And from here, throw myself straight into those big, warm arms.
Will they be startled?
Will they catch me?
The anxiety is still there. I'm still... a little afraid.
But... I've made up my mind.
I will believe in their love, with all my heart.
With that resolve, Satoko...
Broke into the brightest smile she could muster and ran toward the two of them......
``...After that, I don't quite remember... how much time passed...``
``But when I came to...``
``I was lying on the ground.``
``And when I looked up... I saw... a broken railing, horribly twisted.``
``Mom... and my stepdad were...``
``Gone... I couldn't see them anywhere......``
The weight of it stole my voice.
My heart was pounding, and I kept gasping, trying again and again to steady my breathing.
And finally... no, barely... I managed to force the shock and anguish down into the pit of my stomach and speak to Satoko.
``Satoko............!@k@vS14/05/220500227. Is that really... what happened...?!``
``...Honestly, I was never sure about what really happened, until now.@k@vS14/04/220400114. I always hated my stepdad.``
``So I thought it was all lies again, and that the truth was simply that I had killed the two of them...@k@vS14/04/220400116. But --``
``I can clearly remember the last expressions on Mom's and my stepdad's faces.@k@vS14/04/220400118. They were desperately pushing me away, trying to keep me from falling with them...!``
``And then... and then...!@k@vS14/04/220400120. When they realized I was safe... the two of them... they smiled......!!``
From the bed above, I could hear Satoko's sobs.
I wanted to climb out from under here and be at her side -- right now.
To stroke her head, comfort her... to tell her it was all right.
But.
My situation wouldn't allow it... and that filled me with bitter frustration.
changeReason: Old simplifies to 'neither the time nor place' losing the emotional weight; JPN 恨めしかった conveys bitter frustration at one's circumstances, which the new captures.

TextENGNew: ``Satoko... that's enough.@k@vS14/05/220500230. Really, truly... it's enough now...``
``...No, Rika.@k@vS14/04/220400122. Perhaps this is nothing more than a comforting lie my mind created to help me escape...@k@vS14/04/220400124. But in my dream, a voice spoke to me.``
``Those words saved me...@k@vS14/04/220400128. They forgave the sins I had kept locked away deep inside my memories...!``
Satoko's voice was thick with tears, yet it carried a clear, unwavering strength.
``Looking back now... I think that voice might have been yours, Rika.@k@vS14/04/220400130. ...That's why I will protect you, even if it costs me my life.``
``So please, let me keep fighting alongside you from now on...!``
How could anyone say no to that? Once more, I felt a surge of pride at having this small, lovely friend -- one who lived with such brightness and determination.
``Being Satoko's friend...@k@vS14/05/220500234. I'm truly, truly glad for that...``
``As am I, Rika......``
I felt warm tears spilling from the corners of my eyes as I clenched my fists tight.
Twenty-nine hours left. I will endure this -- no matter what...! And together with these wonderful friends, I will seize our happy future...!!
``Good work.@k@vS14/11/220700300. Who was that?``
``Our station chief this time!@k@vS14/19/223100037. They're bringing out the heavy artillery now!``
``But he's no match for me!@k@vS14/19/223100039. I'll show them what a man with true resolve is capable of!``
``Good thing the chief's at the station chiefs' golf outing today!@k@vS14/19/223100041. If he'd shown up in person, that would have been a real headache.``

``Nfu fu fu fu!@k@vS14/11/220700302. Calling from the golf course -- is he working or isn't he?@k@vS14/11/220700303. Ootaka-kun must have quite the silver tongue to get a man on the green to act this fast.``
``Since indirect attacks haven't worked, think they'll try a direct one next?@k@vS14/19/223100043. ...You think Ootaka himself will come?``
``If he can't reach the station chief, he'll have no choice but to come himself.@k@vS14/11/220700305. Almost 8:30.@k@vS14/11/220700306. He'll be here within the hour.``
It was no longer night. Morning had fully arrived.
The cicada chorus had already begun. ...Listening to that alone, you might mistake today for just another ordinary day.
But... today would be anything but ordinary.
changeReason: Both say today would not be ordinary/normal - same meaning.

TextENGNew: Ooishi and his allies had already grasped the tail of a vast conspiracy...
``...Knowing Ootaka-kun's personality, he'll probably march right through the front entrance with his chest puffed out.``
``Heh, typical of someone who thinks he's the only righteous person in the room.``
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ``...All right, let's meet him at the water's edge.@k@vS14/11/220700309. Kuma-chan, hold down the interrogation room.@k@vS14/11/220700310. And have those photographs developed and ready.``
``Almost there...@k@vS14/19/223100046. Oh, just came through now! Here, here!``
...The front desk would be opening soon.
The clerks were assembled behind the counter, waiting for the opening chime.
Having to tangle with that insufferable man this early in the morning...
If only the opponent were a buxom young lady instead. That would have been much nicer.
......Ooishi's role here was critical.
...If Sonozaki Shion were simply handed over to prefectural headquarters, even if she never talked, the damage to her future would be severe.
And the uncertainty surrounding Furude Rika's whereabouts and whether she was alive or dead -- that was the crux of this entire operation.
...Every minute, every second they could buy would keep the enemy off-balance, giving Akasaka and the others time to grab hold of whatever loose threads emerged.
But if the enemy learned Rika was still alive before Akasaka seized those threads, they would simply regroup.
The Alphabet Project would regain credibility, and the enemy would postpone this operation only to return with a more perfected plan.
...This time, Furude Rika had somehow seen through the conspiracy with miraculous intuition, but... that was exactly that -- a miracle. A divine wind.

A miracle is a one-time opportunity, nothing more. If you start expecting another, you've already lost. So... there would be no next time. If they didn't crush the enemy here and now, they would never win.

...They could deal serious damage internally, but... a draw would ultimately amount to the enemy's victory. ...They had to expose the enemy at all costs, let Akasaka strike at the main body, and bring the whole thing crashing down.
No matter how things played out, this affair would never become public. But... there were people right here, right now, who would not be saved unless he put his body on the line...!!
On paper, everything he was doing constituted a violation of duty. ...But... this was the moment to truly set his detective's soul ablaze.
That's right -- hadn't his old mentor knocked him flat and taught him that very lesson, all those years ago...!!
The automatic glass doors slid open... and despite the muggy morning heat, a man in an immaculately tailored suit strode inside.
...He had two lackeys in tow. As always, the man couldn't resist flaunting his authority.
``Well, good morning there, Ootaka-kun!``
``...!@k@vS14/00/222900002. Well, well. Good morning, Ooishi-san.@k@vS14/00/222900003. And I'd prefer you address me properly as 'Ootaka,' not 'Ootaka-kun.'``
``Oh, come now, don't be like that, Ootaka-kun.@k@vS14/11/220700313. After all, we used to sit at the same mahjong table, didn't we?``
Ootaka's expression twisted slightly. ...He had once prided himself on his mahjong prowess, but Ooishi had thoroughly trounced him in front of everyone, leaving his pride in tatters.
``I'd love to reminisce with you,@k@vS14/00/222900006. but I'm here on urgent business today, so if you'll excuse me.``

``Nfu fu fu fu! Oh, come on, don't be like that~!@k@vS14/11/220700315. Remember that inter-station kendo tournament when you forgot to commission the calligraphy for the award certificates and came crying to me?``
``I quietly wrote them backstage for you, and that's the only reason the ceremony went off without a hitch.``
``......Tch.@k@vS14/00/222900008. I-I appreciate that, but it has nothing to do with today's matter.``
``Oh? You appreciated it?@k@vS14/11/220700318. Na ha ha, my apologies.@k@vS14/11/220700319. The story around the department was a bit different, you see.``
``At headquarters, they all believe I begged to write them, and you graciously let me -- isn't that what they say?``
``That's been your story ever since the director questioned why the certificate calligraphy was so poor, hasn't it?``
``...W-well, who knows?@k@vS14/00/222900010. Perhaps someone got the wrong idea...?@k@vS14/00/222900011. ...Anyway, let's move on.``
``Headquarters may be your turf, but this is Okinomiya Station.@k@vS14/11/220700323. Things don't just go your way here.``
``Oh...?@k@vS14/00/222900014. And what exactly do you mean by that, Ooishi-san?``
``I don't know what brings you here, but your jurisdiction is Gogura.@k@vS14/11/220700325. Not Okinomiya.@k@vS14/11/220700326. Ootaka-kun, you should go back to your own station.``
``Wha... under what authority do you--!``
``That's my line. What authority brings you here?``

``I-I'm here in connection with a classified investigation currently being conducted by headquarters.@k@vS14/00/222900017. I've been informed that a key person of interest is being held here, so I've come for a hearing.``

``Then please inquire by telephone.@k@vS14/11/220700329. Okinomiya is a working-class town, you see.@k@vS14/11/220700330. We don't take kindly to men in tailored suits swaggering in first thing in the morning.``
``...I see.@k@vS14/00/222900019. However, there should be an order from the station chief to transfer the suspect to headquarters.``
``Nfu fu fu fu.@k@vS14/11/220700332. For an elite, you don't seem to know the principle of 'one crime, one arrest, one detention'?@k@vS14/11/220700333. Ootaka-kun?``
``We have the authority to detain her at our station for up to 48 hours before prosecution, you know.``
``Then charge her and send her to prosecution at once.``
``Oh, I'd love to, but this particular suspect has an iron jaw.@k@vS14/11/220700336. She's maintaining her silence and won't give us a confession. Quite the tough nut.``
``...Then allow us to sit in on the interrogation.``
``...Wh-...what?!@k@vS14/00/222900023. I am requesting this as the officer in charge of classified cases at headquarters--``
``If that's the truth!!@k@vS14/11/220700340. Then I won't question your detective's spirit.``

``A detective's spirit is a noble thing.@k@vS14/11/220700342. Sure, nobody would do this job if they didn't get paid. That's a given.@k@vS14/11/220700343. But the real question is what you carry with you beyond the salary.``
``...If you truly have that spirit, then I'll step aside.``
Ooishi's eyes blazed with an intensity that bordered on menacing. ...Seeing that look, Ootaka felt a host of fearsome memories he had forgotten come flooding back.
He didn't want Ooishi to sense his fear, but he couldn't suppress the audible gulp he had reflexively swallowed.
``V-very well, then step aside.@k@vS14/00/222900025. I came here driven by my own detective's spirit.@k@vS14/00/222900026. Now make way, please.``
``Then push me aside with that spirit of yours.@k@vS14/11/220700346. Go on.``
``If your detective's spirit weighs as much as mine, brushing me aside should be a trifle.@k@vS14/11/220700348. Come on.@k@vS14/11/220700349. Push me aside, if you can.``
It was an unmistakable declaration: he would not be allowed through.
changeReason: Both say he would not be allowed through - same meaning.

TextENGNew: ...How could such madness be tolerated?! Ootaka was thrown into confusion.
He was an elite at prefectural headquarters. The only things that should matter were academic pedigree and performance evaluations.
He had built his entire career on that belief. This man Ooishi refused to acknowledge any of it, refused to show him the slightest respect -- he was the lowest of the low.
The worst type of person -- a savage who denied every superior to protect his own ego, establishing his worth through nothing but brute force.
It had mystified Ootaka ever since their days together at headquarters. Why did everyone trust this man? Why did they respect him? ...Why did they value him more than me...?
``Y-you don't have the authority to decide what happens in an interrogation!@k@vS14/00/222900030. Do you think someone below the rank of chief inspector can detain a suspect?!``
``Ah, then you'll have to negotiate with our Chief Inspector Mitsuda.@k@vS14/11/220700351. I have his authorization for this interrogation.@k@vS14/11/220700352. Unfortunately, he's out right now.``
``Then call him back, immediately!``

``Call him back, you say?@k@vS14/11/220700354. ...I should warn you, he may be timid, but his convictions are rock-solid.@k@vS14/11/220700355. He'll come back saying the same thing I just told you. Guaranteed.``
``...M-...move aside.@k@vS14/00/222900033. If you don't step away this instant, I can have you charged with obstruction of investigation...!!``
``Oh? And what investigation would that be?@k@vS14/11/220700357. Tell me and I'll gladly cooperate.``
``...How dense can you be?@k@vS14/00/222900035. It's classified. I can't discuss it with some backwater detective like you...!``
``I see. Well, if you can't tell me everything, tell me just one thing.@k@vS14/11/220700359. ...What are you carrying on your shoulders for this job?``
``...Carrying...?@k@vS14/00/222900037. What are you talking about?``
``I'm carrying something.@k@vS14/11/220700361. ...Behind me stand two thousand villagers, and their fates weigh on my shoulders.``
``If I let you through, those two thousand people -- no, far more when you count their families and relatives -- will suffer.@k@vS14/11/220700363. ......Can you... really push aside that weight?``
``...I haven't the faintest idea what you're talking about...!``
``...Fine, I'll lay it bare.@k@vS14/11/220700365. Your master is planning something terrible.@k@vS14/11/220700366. You may not know the details, but you're being used.``
``...That plot is on the verge of collapse, which is why they dragged you away from your comfortable evening at home and ordered you to act immediately.``
``I don't know how much pocket money they're paying you, but... if that's heavier than your detective's spirit, then...``
He stopped there, but... his eyes said the rest without words.
Depending on the answer, he would not hesitate to use force... no, given his willingness to accept disciplinary action, it was entirely possible he would resort to violence.
...This man was enough of a maverick to do exactly that.
``I-I have absolutely no idea what you're talking about...!@k@vS14/00/222900040. Just let me through--``
``...Hold it.@k@vS14/11/220700370. I'm not finished.``
As Ootaka tried to slip past, Ooishi's powerful arm clamped down on his shoulder.
Ootaka tried to wrench free, but the grip was like a vise, impossible to escape...!
``Oh? I'm barely putting any strength into it.@k@vS14/11/220700372. If you had a true detective's spirit, something like this wouldn't faze you.@k@vS14/11/220700373. Come on, try harder.``
``And yet you can't even pry my hand off.@k@vS14/11/220700375. ...Do you know why?``
``...Get off of meeee...!``
``It's because you don't have a detective's soul!!!``
Still gripping his shoulder, Ooishi hurled him away. Ootaka went tumbling across the floor, sliding more than two meters before coming to a stop.
``Wh-... this is obstruction of investigation!@k@vS14/00/222900048. Obstruction of justice -- no, assault, caught in the act!@k@vS14/00/222900049. You two! Arrest this man!``
Ootaka barked the order at his two subordinates, but they seemed utterly at a loss for what to do.
The man in front of them was also a police officer, and clearly there was some deep history between these two. It was hardly their place to intervene.
changeReason: Old truncates the observers' feeling that it wasn't their place to intervene (自分たちの出番とは思えない), though it captures the feud between the two officers.

TextENGNew: ...Moreover... this was their instinct as subordinates speaking. ...The man before them was the superior one. Even if ordered, they couldn't bring themselves to challenge him...!
``Wh-what's going on, Ooishi-san...?!``
Officers gathered in the lobby, wondering what the commotion was about. Their faces showed nothing but confusion.
``Nothing to worry about.@k@vS14/11/220700378. Some greenhorn from headquarters was spouting nonsense, so I gave him a bit of senior guidance.``
The officers looked back and forth between Ooishi and Ootaka. They could tell something serious was happening, but without knowing the details, they had no idea whose side to take.

Ooishi was the dependable elder brother of every officer at Okinomiya Station. Not a single one failed to respect him. If Ooishi said there was no merit to the other man's position, then the man glaring furiously from the floor must be in the wrong.
Ooishi was a man of mature judgment. If he was making a scene like this in a public lobby, then it had to be something truly serious.
Drawing this kind of attention was an almost unbearable humiliation for Ootaka. He masked his humiliation with anger and -- never forgetting to at least feign composure -- rose to his feet.
``...I see.@k@vS14/00/222900051. I understand your position.@k@vS14/00/222900052. I'm not here to play games, either. Let's settle this once and for all.``
``Good spirit.@k@vS14/11/220700380. I'll take you on.``
``Bring it on, greenhorn!!!``
``Ohhhhhhhhhhhhhhh!!!``
``Not enough!! That all you got?!``
``Damn it!!!``

``So that's your policeman's spirit?!!@k@vS14/11/220700384. I have two thousand Hinamizawan villagers resting on my shoulders!!@k@vS14/11/220700385. The lives of two thousand people in my jurisdiction depend on me!!@k@vS14/11/220700386. Why don't you tackle me with a spirit that's strong enough to knock down two thousand people?``
``Ohhhhhhhhhhhhhhh!!!``
It was like watching a sumo wrestler take on a child. No matter how many times Ootaka charged, he was effortlessly sent tumbling.
Ooishi stood with his feet planted as if rooted into the earth, radiating the conviction of a man who truly carried two thousand lives on his back.
After being thrown to the floor three times, Ootaka finally seemed to realize he'd been baited. ...Smoothing down his hair, he caught his breath.
``...Heh, heh heh heh...!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_ootaka1002. It seems I got a bit too heated.``
``Well, trying to reason with a brute like you through civilized means was my mistake to begin with.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_ootaka1004. I came prepared, in any case.``
``Oh my~, is that so?@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1043. I can't say I even noticed.``
``My, my... how surprising.@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1045. To think you actually felt gratitude... will it snow tomorrow, I wonder?``
``Ngh...!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_ootaka1006. B-but no matter how much you resist, as long as you draw a policeman's pay, you're bound by police regulations and your superiors' orders!``
``I came here at the request of the Criminal Affairs department chief.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_ootaka1008. You understand what that means... don't you?``

``Hmm... true enough, if it's a top-down directive from the department chief himself, us lowly detectives have no choice but to obey.@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1047. ...You have documentation, I presume?``
``Of course.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_ootaka1010. Here, take a good look at this.``
Ootaka reached into his inner pocket, pulled out a single document, unfolded it, and thrust it triumphantly toward Ooishi.
Good thing I consulted the department chief as a precaution. ...Ootaka thought to himself with a smug, self-satisfied smirk.

The department chief happened to be a senior alumnus from the same university, and Ootaka benefited greatly from his patronage. The reason an inspector like him could wield such authority in Gogura was entirely thanks to that backing.
Still, Ootaka had his pride, and considered this nothing more than an insurance policy for the worst-case scenario.
That said, against senseless brutes like this, an imperial decree was far more effective. 'Be prepared and you won't worry' -- this was the perfect illustration of that maxim...!
``...I see.@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1050. It says here to give full priority cooperation to your investigation, stamped with the department chief's personal seal.``
``Hmm, hmm, hmm... well, I certainly can't defy that.``
``Heh heh heh heh... glad you understand.``
``Now, please escort me to Sonozaki Shion.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_ootaka1013. And prepare for her transfer.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_ootaka1014. Promptly, if you please.``
``...In that case, I'll play an even higher card.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bc_tomoe1002. Ooishi-san.``
``Oh... ohhh!``
Just then.
From behind Ootaka, a woman in a suit hobbled forward on crutches, her gait halting but purposeful.
Ooishi, who had been desperately wracking his brain for an escape while maintaining a facade of calm, saw her and broke into a triumphant, ear-to-ear grin.
``Na ha ha ha, Tomoe-chaaaan!@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1054. I've been waiting for you! Perfect timing~!@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1055. You're still as reliable as ever, Tomoe-chan!``
``Really... Ooishi-san.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bc_tomoe1004. Didn't I tell you to stop calling me 'Tomoe-chan' when we're on duty?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bc_tomoe1005. It's embarrassing, you know.``
``Oh dear, terribly sorry about that!@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1057. And how are your injuries?``

``Can't you tell just by looking?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bc_tomoe1007. You call me out of the blue with an outrageous request...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bc_tomoe1008. And now I had to rush here overnight, injured as I am.``
``Wh-who are you?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_ootaka1017. We're in the middle of an important discussion. Please wait.``
His momentum shattered, Ootaka made no effort to hide his displeasure as he waved Tomoe away.
...His arrogant gesture made her eyebrow twitch for a split second, but she deliberately let it pass, composed herself, and raised her free hand in a crisp salute.
``I am Chief Nanai, head of the media relations section at the National Police Agency's Public Relations Division.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bc_tomoe1010. A pleasure to make your acquaintance.``
``National Police Agency...?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_ootaka1019. What on earth does a public relations officer want here?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_ootaka1020. Go back to your desk, this has nothing to do with you.``
``Yes, I understand that much.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bc_tomoe1012. --Detective Ooishi, I have a directive from the Commissioner General's Secretariat of the National Police Agency.``

``The suspect currently under your custody has, as of this moment, been designated a key witness in a nationally coordinated investigation.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bc_tomoe1014. Accordingly, her custody will be transferred to us.``
``Please proceed promptly with the handover of all relevant investigation materials.``

``Furthermore, her current detention period at this facility is hereby terminated as of 9:00 AM today, after which she will be transferred to the Aichi Prefectural Police Headquarters, where the investigation task force is based.``
``...This directive was issued under the authorization of the Secretary General of the Commissioner General's Secretariat.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bc_tomoe1018. Detective Ooishi, do you have any objections?``
``...If it's a direct order from the brass at the National Police Agency, then I have no choice.@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1059. It's most regrettable, but I shall comply.``
``W-wait just a moment!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_ootaka1023. My transfer order takes priority!!``
``...No, mine takes precedence.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bc_tomoe1020. Your order has been simultaneously voided by this directive.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bc_tomoe1021. Is there anything else?``
``Tuck your tail between your legs and get out, you spineless fool!!``
``If you want to keep running your mouth, I'll summon you and your department chief to the National Police Agency for a formal inquiry and settle this once and for all!! Got it?!!``
``--Eep...?!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bc_ootaka1025. Gh... uuugh...!!``
Hit full force by a bark so fierce no one would have guessed it from her appearance, Ootaka's face went white with terror, and he scurried out of the station without another word.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: Once he was out of sight... Tomoe cleared her throat, her cheeks flushing red, and bowed her head. '...I apologize for that.'
``I just... lost my temper.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bc_tomoe1026. I really can't stand guys like that... sorry about that.``
``No, no, not at all!@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1061. That was far more satisfying than if I'd punched him myself.@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1062. I'm truly grateful -- are you sure you're all right?``
``Ahaha, I'm fine.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bc_tomoe1028. I still owe you so many debts of gratitude, Ooishi-san.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bc_tomoe1029. I'll repay them one by one.``
Tomoe said that with a bright, cheerful laugh.
...Given the timeline, it had been not merely unreasonable but downright absurd to ask. He'd been prepared to grovel with gratitude if she managed to deliver by evening at the earliest.
The fact that she had arrived first thing in the morning meant she must have rushed through the night without rest. Her effort was truly, deeply appreciated.
Moreover... when he had received the department chief's order, Ooishi had honestly been at his wit's end. He had assumed at best he'd get some support from a section chief.
Without her intervention, Sonozaki Shion would undoubtedly have been taken from them...
``...Also, I've sent the forensic results on that burn victim, along with related materials, to you by both fax and post.``
``Additionally, one of my subordinates discovered a significant clue indicating that the body was not actually Takano Miyo.``
``Hoh, and what clue would that be?``

``It was the dental treatment records.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bc_tomoe1033. The victim had indeed received dental work several months ago, and the dental office had a chart under her name matching the records.``
``...However, when a photo was shown to one of the receptionists at the dental office, they confirmed that the person who came in under Takano's name was a different individual.``
``Then... what does that mean?``
``A simple trick using an insurance card.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bc_tomoe1036. Since there's no photograph on those, all you have to do is present it and claim to be the cardholder.``
``And the person who received treatment under Takano's identity... was the one the culprit burned to death.``
If this was true, Takano's alibi of being already dead would collapse entirely.
Combined with the forensics specialist's findings, they should be able to request an arrest warrant for Takano Miyo...!
``Thank you, Tomoe-chan!@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1068. I think we can pull this off now~ Nfu fu fu fu!!``
``Ahaha, glad I could help.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bc_tomoe1039. Well then, I'll be heading back.``
``...Oh, Ooishi-san?``
``Yes? What is it?``

``...'Those who believe shall be saved' -- some religious figure said that once.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bc_tomoe1042. Right now, I can say with absolute certainty... that those words were true.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bc_tomoe1043. So please, do your best too, Ooishi-san.``
``Thank you, Tomoe-chan.``
And with that, Tomoe departed from Okinomiya Station in a car driven by a fellow detective.
Long after the car had vanished from sight, Ooishi stood there motionless, overcome with emotion...
``Well then... shall I get back to the interrogation?@k@vS14/11/220700427. --Hmm?``
``Ohhh!!``
Ooishi had just taken a relieved stretch when his eyes caught the figure of the person he'd been desperately waiting for, walking in through the entrance.
``Ooohh, Akasaka-san!@k@vS14/11/220700430. I've been waiting for you!!``
``Sorry for the delay.``
``...Regarding that matter -- things are moving, thanks to everything you've provided.``
Ooishi casually led Akasaka to a separate room. After confirming they were alone, Akasaka leaned close and spoke.
``Through a plea bargain, it seems a resolution could come as early as tomorrow.``
``Some extremely powerful figures were involved, so honestly I was worried about how things would go, but...``
changeReason: Both say powerful figures were involved, worried how it would turn out - same meaning.

TextENGNew: ``Oh? Could that be...@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1073. that politician who was just on the news recently?``
``Yes... if someone of that rank were the mastermind, it would certainly explain how they were able to apply pressure to Public Security's investigations.``
``But at first we thought such a powerful man, a future candidate for prime minister, couldn't get involved in such a high risk project.``
``But unsatisfied with that, he was trying to expand his power by monopolizing the funds left by the late political merchant who launched the project...``

``Though those ambitions ended up a pipe dream thanks to this arrest.@k@vS14/13/VT4Bc_akasaka1081. And those who were manipulating Miyo Takano were doing so with the intention of eliminating that politician in an opposition party.``
``...But now that he has been arrested, there's nothing inciting her to take that risk.``
``Therefore, it's likely the people backing Miyo Takano behind the scenes will cut her off and fade away.``
``In the near future, whether she ceases her acts of violence or not, they'll profess that they have nothing to do with her conspiracies.``
``...If she gets forsaken like that, Miyo Takano will learn that carrying out the plan would be meaningless, so she'd be forced to abandon it.``

``In terms of public security, we were able to eliminate the politicians with problematic foreign relations, so we'll tacitly accept their future self-preservation tactics.@k@vS14/13/VT4Bc_akasaka1087. ...At least for this time, though.``
Akasaka cynically smiled as he said that. ...They were probably performing a job similar to Public Security, but there was something he didn't like about them.

``Well, a spear carrier like Takano doesn't know that story yet, so that deterrent won't be effective until tomorrow.@k@vS14/13/221300132. Until then, we'll need to continue physically protecting Rika-chan.``
``Nfu fu fu!@k@vS14/11/220700434. Youngsters are always working harder and faster than me!``
Ooishi happily laughed and his stomach shook as he said that.
As long as those kids are involved, the future of Hinamizawa will be secure.
...Ooishi was nearly at retirement age, so he was thinking that it was time for old soldiers to take their leave, but he still felt like all his hard work in Hinamizawa was worth it.
``O-Ooishi-san!!``
As they left the room, Kumagai dashed over with a distraught look on his face.
``What's going on, Kuma-chan?@k@vS14/11/220700436. If you shout too much out here, you could cause the public to panic.``
``Th-that's not important!@k@vS14/19/223100049. Aah, Akasaka-san is here too!!@k@vS14/19/223100050. Perfect!!@k@vS14/19/223100051. H-hurry, come with me!!``
``Wh-what are you guys doing?!``
It happened so suddenly. An armed Mountain Dogs squadron charged into the clinic with Okonogi at the forefront.
It was daytime on a weekday. Naturally, there were several patients sitting in the waiting room, but they were all forced out with no regard for their anxiety or curiosity about the situation.
And, as they approached the door of the exam room... Irie stood up stunned, then rushed to stand in front of them.
``Wh-where are you going?@k@vS14/10/22100267. I can't allow you to go any further dressed like that!!``
``Wha?! What are you doing?!``

Okonogi called out to his subordinates behind like he wanted them to move an inanimate object, rather than a person. Three of them surrounded Irie on all sides and then forcibly pushed him to the floor.
``D-do you think this sort of violence will be permi... Ngh?!``
``...Lead the way.@k@vS14/14/223400044. Satoko Houjou is here, isn't she?``
It happened after Satoko had fallen asleep, and just as I began to yawn at spending such a boring time here.
A commotion suddenly came from the hallway outside, and I could hear a sound reminiscent of military boots hitting the floor.
I thought I heard a faint scream, but the door wildly swung open before I could determine if it came from Irie.
...From down here, I could only see their feet. I could slightly make out the hem of a white coat right next to a set of military boots. The coat undoubtedly belonged to Irie.
And... the man he was trying to resist had to be...!
``Satoko-chan absolutely needs to remain resting!@k@vS14/10/22100272. And why did you have to rush our patients out of here with so many people?!``
Okonogi's voice rang out with no regard for what Irie was saying. Behind them, I heard the clatter of something being set up, and then brought inside. It was... a basic stretcher.
``Wha...? Are you insane?!@k@vS14/10/22100274. Where on earth are you planning to take her if you get her out of here?!``
``There's no need to answer that.@k@vS14/14/223400047. Just following orders from above. If you have any complaints, direct it to them.``
``Orders from above?!@k@vS14/10/22100276. And who on earth would that be?!``
``The Major.@k@vS14/14/223400049. Take it up with Major Takano.``
As Okonogi said that without any hesitation, Irie froze and looked terrified.
``M-Major Takano...?@k@vS14/10/22100278. That's ridiculous!@k@vS14/10/22100279. You yourself reported that she died just yesterday!``
``...Don't bother feigning ignorance, Lieutenant Colonel.``
``What... Gh?!``
``Director.@k@vS14/14/223400052. You've been suspected of spying.@k@vS14/14/223400053. If you make another attempt to get in our way, it'll be seen as an act of treason. Got it?``
``I figure you're the one who let infomation regarding Rika Furude's disappearance leak to ``Tokyo`` HQ.``
``I almost let your absent-minded appearance fool me. I never thought you'd be the wrench in our gears.@k@vS14/14/223400056. ...Now, take her away.``
As he said that, four men rushed to the bed while pulling the stretcher. However, Irie continued resisting by standing in front of the men with his back toward Satoko in the bed.

``Wh-what do you plan to do after taking her?!@k@vS14/10/22100285. What crime did this girl commit? What on earth did she do?!@k@vS14/10/22100286. Do you think it's acceptable to do something so inhumane?!``

``...Satoko Houjou is a key stakeholder to both R and Shion Sonozaki.@k@vS14/14/223400058. Shion Sonozaki is apparently continuing to keep silent about R's whereabouts,@k@vS14/14/223400059. but what would she do in exchange for this girl...?``
``Th... that's completely shameless!!@k@vS14/10/22100288. If you so much as lay a finger on her, you bastards won't get away with it!!``
...This is bad...!! The situation... has changed...?!
The men surrounded Irie all at once, and forced him down to his knees on the floor. Then, a sound like a striking hammer rang out, along with a breathtaking cry from Irie.
``I'll say it one last time.@k@vS14/14/223400062. ...Don't get in our way.``
``......I-I'm... still a doctor!!@k@vS14/10/22100291. I can't accept this... even if it costs me my life!!``
Oh no... Irie...!
Run away...! I'm begging you, go...!!
But that moment.
``...Would you keep quiet?@k@vS14/04/220400135. It's noisy and I'm having trouble sleeping...``
Just barely,
I could hear a squeaking sound coming from the mattress overhead.
No way... Satoko...?!
``S-Satoko-chan...?``
``...Hoh, so she's regained consciousness?@k@vS14/14/223400066. What's the meaning of that, Director...?``
``I've awoken just now.@k@vS14/04/VT4Bc_satoko1029. With all this racket, even the dead would jump up in shock...!``
As she said that,
Satoko slowly got up from the bed and stood with her feet right in front of me.

``...Do you not feel ashamed of yourselves?@k@vS14/04/220400139. Gathering a bunch of gentlemen that look like soldiers inside this tiny clinic.@k@vS14/04/220400140. ...There's a saying about making a mountain out of a molehill.``
``What's with this girl...?!``
``Why are you all looking at a little girl like something frightening?!@k@vS14/04/VT4Bc_satoko1031. If you wish to take me somewhere, you can do so without making threats!!``
``The coach doesn't know anything and he's not double-dealing you, so stop resorting to violence against him!!``
Satoko spoke in a resolute voice. Her vigor must've distracted them because the men looked daunted and caught their breath, then lowered their guns and stood at the ready.
``...Take her away.``
On Okonogi's signal, the men rushed over to the left and right of Satoko. Without resisting, Satoko slowly... but powerfully stepped forward.
Oh no...!
Don't go, Satoko...!!
They'll kill you if you go! I'm certain of it!!
Weren't you the one who said so?
You said you were truly glad to be my friend!!
I feel the same way. I don't want you to die!!
So don't go! Don't die! You can't!
If you're going to die, then right here I'll...!
*bang*
Satoko stomped her foot on the floor right in front of me. I couldn't help but gasp and barely suppressed the sound, but... I realized what she meant in an instant.
Satoko... was telling me ``don't come out`` ...!!
``...What's wrong?``
``Nothing at all.@k@vS14/04/220400145. My legs are still a little unsteady, so I was just warming them up.``
I want to jump out there.
I want to rush out and say ``take me in Satoko's place.``
However.
That would mean the end of everything.
But... if that's the case... then I...!!
Can I really abandon my best friend... when she's standing right before my eyes...!?!
``S-Satoko-chan...!``
``Coach, thank you for all you've done.@k@vS14/04/VT4Bc_satoko1034. ...Give my regards to Nii-nii and everyone else.``
``That you merely feel that way is enough...@k@vS14/04/220400149. I have no regrets about giving my life if it means protecting my friends...``
...I understand.
Her words were directed not just at the coach, but also toward one other person in the room...
I... can't do anything?
If I reach my hand out, it'll reach her. But I have no choice but to shrink back here...?!
``...Director Irie, I'll ask for more details later.@k@vS14/14/223400070. Come on, escort the director to another room.``
Okonogi and the others left the room with Irie.
And the only one left in the room... was me.
It was completely silent inside the exam room.
Blood came overflowing out of my mouth.
I squeezed my fists so tight that my nails dug into my palms, and the pain and blood slowly spread.
Right there...
I was filled with regret, sorrow, and shame... but I desperately concealed my voice... and couldn't do anything but weep.
